qa_driven_change.source_note: W69_LOCAL_CANNY_EYEONLY_MULTISEED_ROBUSTNESS_VISUAL_QA_20260707T092500-0500 noted a faint right-edge vertical seam/background artifact in seed 711570105.
qa_driven_change.request_change: Retested seed 711570105 with explicit continuous-background and no right-edge-seam wording, plus Canny strength lowered from 0.45 to 0.42 and end_percent shortened from 0.65 to 0.60.
qa_driven_change.minimum_rerun_scope: one local sample from the changed request only

improvements: The prior faint dotted vertical right-edge seam is no longer visible.
The new background reads as a continuous neutral matte field rather than a right-edge panel or strip.
Eyes remain readable and aligned after the lower/shorter Canny guidance change.
Jaw, neck, shoulder, and blazer boundaries remain coherent without visible Canny leakage.
tradeoffs_or_notes: The lower Canny setting slightly changes wardrobe/color presentation into a lighter blazer, but the image stays clothed and coherent.
Hands, full body, contact points, and broad scene robustness remain outside this local minimum rerun.

candidate_status_update: The seam-suppression rerun improves the noted robustness seed artifact and can be used as supporting local Canny evidence, but it does not replace the preferred seed 711570102 candidate without broader multi-seed retesting.
remaining_risks: This is a single local rerun and not final MOD-17 certification.
Target-runtime EC2 object_info/path/hash/input proof, bounded generation, pullback, technical QA, and strict visual QA remain separate promotion gates.
Hands, full-body anatomy, contact points, and wider prompt robustness are not certified.
next_action: Keep Canny local status as pass-with-notes with seam-suppression improvement recorded; continue local-first on another named implementation/QA gap or intentionally select target-runtime proof only when required.
